children.heading: Delegation of authority pertaining to certification of expenditures
children.label: a Delegation of authority pertaining to certification of expenditures

children.text: subject to subsection (b), make expenditures, from such amounts as may be specifically appropriated therefor, for unforeseen emergencies arising in the diplomatic and consular service and, to the extent authorized in appropriation Acts, funds expended for such purposes may be accounted for in accordance with ; andsection 3526(e) of title 31
delegate to subordinate officials the authority vested in him by pertaining to certification of expenditures.section 3526(e) of title 31

children.label: A
children.text: serve to further the realization of foreign policy objectives;

children.label: B
children.text: are a matter of urgency to implement;

children.label: C
children.text: with respect to activities the expenditures for which are required to be certified under subsection (a), require confidentiality in the best interests of the conduct of foreign policy by the United States; and

children.label: D
children.text: are not otherwise prohibited by law.

children.label: i
children.text: United States Government employees and their dependents; and

children.label: ii
children.text: private United States citizens or third-country nationals, on a reimbursable basis to the maximum extent practicable, with such reimbursements to be credited to the applicable Department of State appropriation and to remain available until expended, except that no reimbursement under this clause shall be paid that is greater than the amount the person evacuated would have been charged for a reasonable commercial air fare immediately prior to the events giving rise to the evacuation;

children.label: B
children.text: loans made to destitute citizens of the United States who are outside the United States and made to provide for the return to the United States of its citizens;

children.label: C
children.text: visits by foreign chiefs of state or heads of government to the United States;

children.label: D
children.text: travel of delegations representing the President at any inauguration or funeral of a foreign dignitary;

children.label: E
children.text: travel of the President, the Vice President, or a Member of Congress to a foreign country, including advance arrangements, escort, and official entertainment;

children.label: F
children.text: travel of the Secretary of State within the United States and outside the United States, including official entertainment;

children.label: G
children.text: official representational functions of the Secretary of State and other principal officers of the Department of State;

children.label: H
children.text: official functions outside the United States the expenses for which are not otherwise covered by amounts appropriated for representation allowances;

children.label: I
children.text: investigations and apprehension of groups or individuals involved in fraudulent issuance of United States passports and visas; and

children.label: J
children.text: gifts of nominal value given by the President, Vice President, or Secretary of State to a foreign dignitary.

children.heading: Annual confidential audit and report
children.label: c Annual confidential audit and report
children.text: The Inspector General of the Department of State shall conduct a periodic audit of the Department of State’s emergency expenditures and prepare and transmit to the Speaker of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate an annual report indicating whether such expenditures were made in accordance with subsections (a) and (b) of this section.
require the borrower to provide a verifiable address and social security number at the time of application;
require a written loan agreement which includes a repayment schedule;
bar passports from being issued or renewed for those individuals who are in default;
refer any loan more than one year past due to the Department of Justice for litigation;
obtain addresses from the Internal Revenue Service for all delinquent accounts which have social security numbers;
report defaults to commercial credit bureaus as provided in ;section 3711(e) of title 31
be permitted to use any funds necessary to contract with commercial collection agencies, notwithstanding section 3718(c) of title 31;1See References in Text note below.1
charge interest on all loans as of , with the rate of interest to be that set forth in ;May 1, 1983section 3717(a) of title 31
assess charges, in addition to the interest provided for in paragraph (8), to cover the costs of processing and handling delinquent claims, as of ;May 1, 1983
assess a penalty charge, in addition to the interest provided for in paragraphs (8) and (9), of 6 per centum per year for failure to pay any portion of a debt more than ninety days past due; and
implement the interest and penalty provisions in paragraphs (8), (9), and (10) for all current and future loans, regardless of whether the debts were incurred before or after .May 1, 1983
